reasoning: Zohran Mamdani is running in the general election for Mayor of NYC in 2025 as the Democratic nominee, facing mostly Andrew Cuomo (Independent) and Curtis Sliwa (Republican). For him to finish exactly in second place, he would almost certainly need to lose the election to Cuomo, considering Sliwa is polling significantly behind. Currently, all polling from October 2025 indicates Mamdani holds a substantial lead, around 43-46% compared to Cuomo's 29-33%. Further validating this, an October 23 Reddit post indicates that Polymarket places his chance of winning first place at 94%. Conversely, bookmakers and earlier prediction markets have assigned Cuomo roughly a 6-10% chance of an upset victory. Since any scenario in which Mamdani does not win entails him almost identically capturing the second-most votes, his probability of placing second is effectively equal to his probability of a loss. Factoring in current prediction market dynamics and standard polling error margins down to the final weeks, I assess his probability of getting second place to be roughly 6%.
